# Imagined Inventory: ai-chat-endpoints

**Project:** tradeguruelectrical
**Created:** 2026-03-14
**Generated by:** `/imagine-inventory`
**Vision:** Centralised API relay at `tradeguru.com/api/v1/*` serving iOS, Android, and Web clients. Uses OpenAI Responses API as the primary AI surface, with dedicated endpoints for files, audio, and images. All clients receive the same structured `{ blocks: [...] }` JSON contract. The relay hides the AI engine — today OpenAI, tomorrow a custom model — so clients never change.

---

## Instructions

This is a **forward-looking TODO inventory** — none of these items exist yet. It represents every API endpoint, model, service, and component needed to deliver the full AI chat flow.

### How to use this document
- Items are numbered sequentially within each sub-type (restart at 1 per section)
- Status tracks build progress: `todo` → `in-progress` → `done` → `cut` (descoped)
- Run `/imagine-inventory` again to generate additional inventories for new features
- As items get built, update status and add the file path
- Cross-reference with `/get-screen-inventory chat` to track what's wired on the chat screen
- Cross-reference with `2-analytics-relay.md` for the relay architecture that wraps these endpoints

### Document sections
1. **Summary** — Counts per sub-type with status breakdown
2. **Methodology** — API surface decision and relay architecture
3. **API Endpoints** — Every OpenAI endpoint the relay calls internally
4. **Structured JSON Response Contract** — The `{ blocks: [...] }` cross-platform contract
5. **Inventory** — Complete itemised file list by sub-type
6. **Dependency Graph** — What blocks what
7. **Build Order** — Recommended implementation sequence
8. **Environment Requirements** — Keys, config, external services needed
9. **StockTake** — Audit trail

### Status definitions
| Status | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| `todo` | Not started — needs to be built |
| `in-progress` | Currently being worked on |
| `done` | Built and working — file path noted |
| `cut` | Descoped — decided not to build |
| `blocked` | Can't start until dependency is done |

## Methodology

> **Factchecked:** 2026-03-14 via Codex against live OpenAI docs. See `docs/openai-endpoint-factcheck-2.md`.

### API Surface Decision

**Use `POST /v1/responses` (Responses API) as the primary surface for chat, vision, structured outputs, and file_search.**

OpenAI's current Chat Completions docs explicitly state: *"Starting a new project? Use the Responses API."* Since the relay hides the OpenAI surface from all clients, there is no compatibility reason to use Chat Completions for new flows.

**Architecture:**
1. Relay routes stay stable at `tradeguru.com/api/v1/*` — clients never see OpenAI URLs
2. Relay uses Responses API internally for chat, vision, and vector search
3. Relay uses dedicated OpenAI endpoints for files, vector stores, STT, TTS, and image generation
4. Relay normalises all AI output to the `{ blocks: [...] }` contract before returning to clients
5. Swapping the AI engine (e.g. to a fine-tuned model) means changing relay internals only — zero client changes

**Chat Completions (`POST /v1/chat/completions`)** is kept as a documented compatibility fallback. The existing Expo web app uses it via the Assistants API. Migration to Responses will happen as the relay is built.

### Model Selection (as of March 2026)

| Purpose | Model | Notes |
|---------|-------|-------|
| **Primary chat** | `gpt-4o` | Versatile multimodal, $2.50/1M input, $10/1M output. Proven. |
| **Upgrade path** | `gpt-4.1` | Smartest non-reasoning model. Consider for complex fault diagnosis. |
| **Future flagship** | `gpt-5.1` / `gpt-5.4` | GPT-5 series available. Evaluate when cost/benefit makes sense. |
| **STT** | `gpt-4o-transcribe` | Replaces `whisper-1`. Better accuracy. Fallback: `gpt-4o-mini-transcribe` (cheaper). |
| **TTS** | `gpt-4o-mini-tts` | Replaces `tts-1`. Better quality. Fallback: `tts-1` (cheaper). |
| **Images** | `gpt-image-1.5` | Latest image model. Fallback: `gpt-image-1` (cheaper), `gpt-image-1-mini` (cheapest). |

All model selections are relay-side config — clients never specify models.

---

## API Endpoints Required

The complete flow: **Client → Relay (`tradeguru.com/api/v1/*`) → OpenAI → Relay → `{ blocks: [...] }` → Client**

### Core Chat (Responses API — primary surface)

| # | Endpoint | Method | Purpose | Request Shape | Response Shape |
|---|----------|--------|---------|--------------|----------------|
| 1 | `api.openai.com/v1/responses` | POST (streaming) | Main AI chat — relay sends user message + system prompt (per mode) + conversation history. Returns structured JSON content blocks. Streaming via SSE. | `{ model: "gpt-4o", input: [...], text: { format: { type: "json_schema", name: "structured_response", strict: true, schema: { type: "object", properties: { blocks: { type: "array", items: ContentBlockSchema } }, required: ["blocks"] } } }, stream: true }` | SSE stream of response events. Output items include `{ type: "message", content: [{ type: "output_text", text: "{\"blocks\":[...]}" }] }`. Stream ends when all output items are complete. |
| 2 | `api.openai.com/v1/responses` | POST (non-streaming) | Quick structured generation for single-shot queries (e.g. "classify this image") | Same as above but `stream: false` | `{ id: "resp_...", output: [{ type: "message", content: [{ type: "output_text", text: "{\"blocks\":[...]}" }] }], usage: { input_tokens: N, output_tokens: N, total_tokens: N } }` |

> **Compatibility note:** `POST /v1/chat/completions` still works for streaming and non-streaming chat. Structured outputs via `response_format: { type: "json_schema", json_schema: { name, strict: true, schema } }` are still valid on Chat Completions. SSE chunks are `chat.completion.chunk` objects ending with `data: [DONE]`. If `stream_options.include_usage` is enabled, a usage chunk appears before `[DONE]`. The relay may use Chat Completions as a fallback but should default to Responses for new flows.

### Vector Store (RAG — Retrieval Augmented Generation)

| # | Endpoint | Method | Purpose | Request Shape | Response Shape |
|---|----------|--------|---------|--------------|----------------|
| 3 | `api.openai.com/v1/vector_stores` | POST | Create vector store (one-time setup for electrical knowledge base) | `{ name: "tradeguru-electrical", file_ids: [...], expires_after: { anchor: "last_active_at", days: 365 }, chunking_strategy: { type: "auto" }, metadata: { project: "tradeguru" } }` | `{ id: "vs_...", object: "vector_store", name: "...", status: "completed", file_counts: { in_progress: 0, completed: N, failed: 0, cancelled: 0, total: N }, usage_bytes: N, created_at: timestamp, last_active_at: timestamp }` |
| 4 | `api.openai.com/v1/vector_stores/{vs_id}/files` | POST | Attach uploaded file to vector store. Also supports batch upload via `/file_batches`. | `{ file_id: "file-..." }` (required). Optional: `{ attributes: { key: "value" }, chunking_strategy: { type: "auto" } }` | `{ id: "vsf_...", object: "vector_store.file", vector_store_id: "vs_...", status: "completed", usage_bytes: N, created_at: timestamp }` |
| 5 | `api.openai.com/v1/files` | POST (multipart) | Upload a file to OpenAI for vector store or chat attachment. Limits: 512 MB per file, 100 GB per org. | `FormData: { purpose: "assistants", file: <binary> }` | `{ id: "file-...", object: "file", filename: "...", bytes: N, purpose: "assistants", created_at: timestamp }` |
| 6 | `api.openai.com/v1/responses` | POST | Chat with `file_search` tool enabled — AI auto-searches the vector store when the question needs reference material. Uses Responses API for native tool support (recommended). | `{ model: "gpt-4o", input: "What does AS3000 say about neutral connections?", tools: [{ type: "file_search", vector_store_ids: ["vs_..."], max_num_results: 5 }], include: ["file_search_call.results"] }` | `{ id: "resp_...", output: [{ type: "file_search_call", id: "fs_...", status: "completed", results: [{ file_id: "file-...", filename: "AS3000.pdf", text: "Clause 4.3.2 requires..." }] }, { type: "message", content: [{ type: "output_text", text: "{\"blocks\":[...]}" }] }] }` |

> **Note on file_search:** Both Responses API and Chat Completions now support `file_search`. Responses is preferred because its output model makes tool calls and results explicit. The `include: ["file_search_call.results"]` parameter is still required to get raw search excerpts — without it, only the AI's synthesised answer is returned. Additional search controls available: `max_num_results`, `filters`, and ranking options.

> **Note on file uploads:** Accepted file types for vector store / file_search depend on the feature. Validate against the current OpenAI file-search supported format list at implementation time rather than hardcoding a whitelist here.

### Image/Photo Upload (Vision)

| # | Endpoint | Method | Purpose | Request Shape | Response Shape |
|---|----------|--------|---------|--------------|----------------|
| 7 | `api.openai.com/v1/responses` | POST | User sends a photo of a circuit/panel — AI analyses it and returns structured diagnosis. Uses Responses API for new multimodal flows. | `{ model: "gpt-4o", input: [{ role: "user", content: [{ type: "input_text", text: "What's wrong with this circuit?" }, { type: "input_image", image_url: "data:image/jpeg;base64,...", detail: "high" }] }], text: { format: { type: "json_schema", name: "structured_response", strict: true, schema: BlocksSchema } }, stream: true }` | Same streaming output as endpoint #1. Vision analysis embedded as structured blocks (text, warning, step_list, etc.) |

> **Compatibility note:** Vision also works on Chat Completions with `messages[].content` containing `{ type: "image_url", image_url: { url, detail } }`. Combining vision + structured output (`json_schema` response_format) in the same Chat Completions request is valid. For new builds, Responses is the recommended multimodal surface.

### Speech-to-Text (STT)

| # | Endpoint | Method | Purpose | Request Shape | Response Shape |
|---|----------|--------|---------|--------------|----------------|
| 8 | `api.openai.com/v1/audio/transcriptions` | POST (multipart) | User taps Speak → records audio → sends to STT → gets text → feeds into chat | `FormData: { model: "gpt-4o-transcribe", file: <audio.m4a>, language: "en" }` | `{ text: "The circuit breaker keeps tripping..." }` |

> **Note on STT models:** `gpt-4o-transcribe` is the recommended default (better accuracy). `gpt-4o-mini-transcribe` is the cheaper alternative. `gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize` adds speaker labelling. `whisper-1` remains available as a compatibility fallback.
>
> **Supported audio formats:** flac, mp3, mp4, mpeg, mpga, m4a, ogg, wav, webm. **Max file size:** 25 MB.

### Text-to-Speech (TTS)

| # | Endpoint | Method | Purpose | Request Shape | Response Shape |
|---|----------|--------|---------|--------------|----------------|
| 9 | `api.openai.com/v1/audio/speech` | POST | Read AI response aloud — converts text blocks to spoken audio | `{ model: "gpt-4o-mini-tts", input: "The issue is a loose neutral...", voice: "nova", response_format: "aac" }` | Binary audio stream (AAC), play via AVAudioPlayer. Response header `Content-Type: audio/aac`. |

> **Note on TTS models:** `gpt-4o-mini-tts` is the recommended default (newer, better quality). `tts-1` and `tts-1-hd` remain available as cheaper fallbacks. `response_format: "aac"` is still valid.
>
> **Voices:** Refresh the voice list from the live OpenAI speech reference before hardcoding validation in the relay. The docs expose an expanded voice set beyond the original six. `nova` remains valid and is the recommended default.

### Image Generation (optional — for diagram generation)

| # | Endpoint | Method | Purpose | Request Shape | Response Shape |
|---|----------|--------|---------|--------------|----------------|
| 10 | `api.openai.com/v1/images/generations` | POST | Generate wiring diagrams or visual aids on demand | `{ model: "gpt-image-1.5", prompt: "...", size: "1024x1024", output_format: "png", quality: "medium", background: "auto", n: 1 }` | `{ data: [{ b64_json: "..." }] }` |

> **Note on image models:** `gpt-image-1.5` is the latest image generation model. `gpt-image-1` is the previous model, `gpt-image-1-mini` is the cheapest variant. Use `output_format` (NOT `response_format`). Additional parameters: `output_compression`, `moderation`, `user`.

---

## Structured JSON Response Contract

The system prompt (built server-side by the relay's SystemPromptBuilder) instructs the AI to return ALL responses as a JSON object with a `blocks` array. This is the **cross-platform contract** — same JSON renders on iOS (SwiftUI), Android (Compose), and Web (React).

> **Important:** The response is always `{ "blocks": [...] }` — a wrapper object, NOT a bare array. This matches the `json_schema` with `strict: true` used in endpoints #1, #2, and #7. All parsers must expect and unwrap this envelope.

```json
{
  "blocks": [
    { "type": "text", "content": "The issue is likely a loose neutral connection." },
    { "type": "heading", "content": "Diagnosis Steps", "level": 2 },
    { "type": "step_list", "title": "How to fix", "steps": [
      "Turn off the breaker at the main panel",
      "Remove the outlet cover plate",
      "Check the neutral (white) wire connection"
    ]},
    { "type": "warning", "severity": "danger", "content": "Always verify power is off with a non-contact voltage tester before touching any wires." },
    { "type": "code", "language": "wiring", "content": "Black → Hot (L)\nWhite → Neutral (N)\nGreen → Earth (G)" },
    { "type": "parts_list", "items": [
      { "name": "Wire nut", "spec": "yellow, 18-12 AWG", "qty": 2 },
      { "name": "Non-contact voltage tester", "spec": "CAT III 600V", "qty": 1 }
    ]},
    { "type": "regulation", "code": "AS/NZS 3000:2018", "clause": "4.3.2", "summary": "All neutral connections must be mechanically secured." },
    { "type": "callout", "style": "tip", "content": "Take a photo before disconnecting any wires so you have a reference." },
    { "type": "table", "headers": ["Wire", "Color", "Purpose"], "rows": [
      ["Active", "Red/Brown", "Line voltage"],
      ["Neutral", "Black/Blue", "Return path"],
      ["Earth", "Green/Yellow", "Safety ground"]
    ]},
    { "type": "diagram_ref", "query": "RCD wiring diagram single phase", "caption": "Typical RCD wiring layout" },
    { "type": "tool_result", "tool": "file_search", "status": "found", "source": "AS3000_2018.pdf", "excerpt": "Clause 4.3.2 requires..." },
    { "type": "image", "base64": "...", "caption": "Generated wiring diagram", "alt": "Single phase RCD connection" }
  ]
}
```

## Build Order

### Phase 1 — Foundation (models + protocols)

| # | Item | Type | Why first |
|---|------|------|-----------|
| 1 | ContentBlock | model | Everything renders from this — the JSON contract |
| 2 | MessageRole | model | Trivial enum, needed by ChatMessage |
| 3 | ChatMessage | model | Every view and service depends on messages |
| 4 | ChatAttachment | model | Needed for photo/file upload flow |
| 5 | AIStreamEvent | model | Needed for streaming parser |
| 6 | ToolCall | model | Needed for vector search tool calls |
| 7 | AIServiceError | model | Error handling everywhere |
| 8 | AudioTranscription | model | Needed for speech service |
| 9 | VectorSearchResult | model | Needed for file_search results |
| 10 | AIRequest | model | Simplified for relay — messages, mode, device_id |
| 11 | AIServiceProtocol | protocol | Interface before implementation |
| 12 | FileServiceProtocol | protocol | Interface before implementation |
| 13 | AudioServiceProtocol | protocol | Interface before implementation |

### Phase 2 — Services (relay client layer)

| # | Item | Type | Why now |
|---|------|------|---------|
| 1 | DeviceIdentity | model | Needed by all relay services for auth header |
| 2 | StreamParser | utility | Needed before chat service — parses SSE |
| 3 | ImageCompressor | utility | Needed before file service — compresses uploads |
| 4 | RelayChatService | service | Core — calls relay for chat + vision. Wire to send button. |
| 5 | RelayFileService | service | Upload photos/files via relay |
| 6 | RelayAudioService | service | Speech-to-text + text-to-speech via relay |
| 7 | RelayImageService | service | Diagram generation via relay (lower priority) |

### Phase 3 — UI (views + blocks)

| # | Item | Type | Why now |
|---|------|------|---------|
| 1 | BlockRenderer | utility | Dispatch all block types to views |
| 2 | TextBlockView | block | Most common block |
| 3 | HeadingBlockView | block | Section structure |
| 4 | StepListView | block | Core for electrical procedures |
| 5 | WarningCardView | block | Critical for safety alerts |
| 6 | CodeBlockView | block | Wire color codes, terminal labels |
| 7 | PartsListView | block | Materials lists |
| 8 | RegulationView | block | Code compliance |
| 9 | CalloutView | block | Tips and notes |
| 10 | TableBlockView | block | Spec comparisons |
| 11 | ToolResultView | block | Vector search results |
| 12 | DiagramRefView | block | Reference diagrams |
| 13 | ImageBlockView | block | Generated diagrams |
| 14 | StreamingIndicator | view | Loading state |
| 15 | MessageBubble | view | Renders one message |
| 16 | MessageList | view | Renders all messages |

### Phase 4 — Wiring (connect to existing UI)

| # | Item | Type | Why last |
|---|------|------|---------|
| 1 | Wire ChatView send button | wiring | Connect to RelayChatService |
| 2 | Wire ChatInputBar attachment | wiring | Connect to RelayFileService |
| 3 | Wire ChatInputBar speak | wiring | Connect to RelayAudioService |
| 4 | Wire mode switch | wiring | Mode sent to relay → relay builds system prompt server-side |
| 5 | Add MessageList to ChatView | wiring | Replace empty state with messages |

---

## Environment Requirements

| Variable | Required By | Where To Get It | Status |
|----------|------------|-----------------|--------|
| `RELAY_URL` | All iOS services | `https://tradeguru.com/api/v1` — already deployed on Vercel | todo (set in build config) |
| `DEVICE_ID` | All iOS services | Generated on-device (UUID), persisted to Keychain | todo (auto-generated) |

> **Server-side config (lives on Vercel, NOT in the iOS app):** `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_MODEL` (default: `gpt-4o`), `OPENAI_VECTOR_STORE_ID`, `OPENAI_TTS_VOICE` (default: `nova`), `OPENAI_TTS_MODEL` (default: `gpt-4o-mini-tts`), `OPENAI_STT_MODEL` (default: `gpt-4o-transcribe`), `OPENAI_IMAGE_MODEL` (default: `gpt-image-1.5`). See `2-analytics-relay.md` for the full backend config list.

---

## StockTake

| Date | Action | Total | Todo | Done | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|------|------|-------|
| 2026-03-14 | initial imagination | 41 | 41 | 0 | 10 endpoints, 12 block types, 5 services, 3 protocols, 10 models |
| 2026-03-14 | codex factcheck #1 corrections | 41 | 41 | 0 | Fixed: endpoint 6 → Responses API, endpoint 7 +model param, endpoint 10 gpt-image params, JSON contract standardised to `{ blocks: [] }`, added `strict: true`, expanded response shapes, added upgrade notes for STT/TTS |
| 2026-03-14 | codex factcheck #2 — full rewrite | 38 | 38 | 0 | **23 changes applied.** Methodology: Responses API as primary surface. Models: STT→gpt-4o-transcribe, TTS→gpt-4o-mini-tts, Images→gpt-image-1.5. Services: renamed OpenAI*→Relay* (all calls go through tradeguru.com relay). SystemPromptBuilder moved to backend. Config: removed OPENAI_API_KEY/MODEL from iOS, added RELAY_URL + DEVICE_ID. AIRequest simplified (no model/tools/response_format — relay handles). Count reduced 41→38 (SystemPromptBuilder moved to inventory #2, 2 config items removed). |
